headline: All majors underwater on the year, all below the 200-day line, yet whales are moving coins to cold storage and funding remains positive across the board.
paragraphs: The regime is uniformly bearish on structure. Bitcoin sits at 64,961, down 47.9 percent from its 12-month high and below the 200-day moving average. Ethereum, at 1,924, is 60.1 percent down from peak and below the same line. Solana, XRP, BNB, Doge, and Cardano all share the same shape: every single major asset is below its 200-day SMA, and none has a golden cross. There is no divergence here, no hidden strength in the technicals. The long-term trend is down, and the structure confirms it across the entire tape.

Momentum shows a split. Bitcoin's RSI sits at 56.2 with MACD above signal, a mild neutral posture, while its 24-hour change is flat at minus 0.02 percent. Ethereum is the outlier: RSI at 67.1 with MACD above signal and up 1.81 percent on the day, suggesting it is holding a bit of short-term heat. Solana's MACD is below signal with RSI at 52.6, a weaker picture. Doge is the lowest energy: RSI at 41.6, down 0.66 percent, and below its 50 level. Most of the majors show MACD above signal despite the trend environment, which means momentum oscillators are trying to recover, but they are doing so from a deeply depressed structural foundation. It is a recovery attempt, not a reversal.

Positioning on leverage markets is long-heavy and still charging buyers. Bitcoin's funding sits at 0.72 basis points per 8 hours, or 7.9 percent annualized, with a long-short ratio of 1.24, meaning longs outnumber shorts by a quarter. Open interest is 1.98 billion USD, substantial but not extreme. In the last 23 hours, 1,518 liquidations cleared 3.0 million in long positions and 7.4 million in shorts, a 2.4-to-1 ratio favoring the shorts. Ethereum's funding is lighter at 0.45 basis points 8-hourly (5.0 annualized), its ratio is 1.1, and shorts are being liquidated harder: 8.1 million in shorts versus 4.6 million in longs over 8 hours. Solana's funding has collapsed to 0.09 basis points (1.0 annualized), its ratio is 2.37 favoring longs, and liquidations are near balanced. Doge is the wildcard: funding at 1.0 basis points (11.0 annualized), ratio at 4.3 longs to shorts, a crowded unilateral bet. The market is still paying to be long across most assets, but the energy is cooling, and shorts are being caught on the wrong side.

Whale behavior is the tape's dominant tell. In the last 24 hours, 82.6 million USD in Bitcoin moved off exchange onto what the data labels as cold storage or private custody, a net outflow via OKEx. Over the past four weeks, the weekly flow trend reads: plus 199.8 million, plus 1.76 billion, plus 1.13 billion, then minus 135 million. Three weeks of strong accumulation into cold wallets, followed by a pullback, now resumed. Stablecoin float sits at 306.8 billion USD, down 1.83 percent over 30 days, a slow drain of dry powder. ETF flows into Bitcoin and Ethereum remain steady and positive: 181.1 million and 58.3 million on July 14, cumulative of 51.1 billion and 11.0 billion respectively. The picture here is one of large holders moving coins away from trading venues while traditional finance continues to bid spot products. That combination is not a sign of imminent capitulation; it is a sign of composition shift.

Sentiment is extreme fear at 25, the lowest end of its recent 11-to-28 range, yet this does not match the behavior in the tape. The crowd is panicked, but the big money is not selling into the panic, and funding costs are not collapsing. Bitcoin dominance remains sticky at 56.3 percent with total market cap up 0.67 percent on the day. The gap between what the crowd feels and what the largest accounts are doing is the headline. Fear at this extreme usually occurs when price has found a local bottom, not when it is still hunting for one, but structure says the downtrend has not confirmed a reversal.

Watch for three conditions: a break and close above the 200-day moving average on Bitcoin or Ethereum, which would be the first structural signal of regime change. A sharp reversal in stablecoin float, from outflow to inflow, which would signal the dry powder is being deployed. And a funding flip to negative, which would mean longs are starting to pay shorts to hold, a sign the positioning has turned fragile. Until those flip, the tape shows structure weakness, positioning longs, and whale accumulation into the weakness, a classic pattern with no direction confirmed.
